summary: Matched the common fighter cliff attack code by moving the 09AE14-09AFD4 routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new ftCo_CliffAttack.c/.h pair, wiring the new object into obj_files.mk, and updating neighboring cliff wait/climb files to include the new header. The matched file report shows 100% for ftCo_8009AE14, ftCo_8009AE38, ftCo_8009AEA4, ftCo_CliffAttack_Anim, ftCo_CliffAttack_IASA, ftCo_CliffAttack_Phys, ftCo_CliffAttack_Coll, and ftCo_8009AFD4.
what_changed: Removed the matched ftCo_CliffAttack-related assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, including ftCo_8009AE14, ftCo_8009AE38, ftCo_8009AEA4, ftCo_CliffAttack_Anim, ftCo_CliffAttack_IASA, ftCo_CliffAttack_Phys, ftCo_CliffAttack_Coll, and ftCo_8009AFD4.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffAttack.c with C implementations for the cliff attack transition checks, motion-state setup, and simple callback wrappers.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffAttack.h declaring the public cliff attack entry points and action callbacks.
Added ftCo_CliffAttack.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Updated ftCo_CliffWait.c and ftCo_CliffClimb.c to include ftCo_CliffAttack.h.

Refined ft_08A4.h prototypes for related helper predicates ftCo_800DF6F8 and ftCo_800DF72C from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to bool ftCo_Fighter* signatures, removed ftCo_8009AFD4 and the now-moved 09AE14/09AE38/09AEA4 declarations from that legacy header, and changed ftCo_8009B040 to bool ftCo_GObj*.
smart_moves: Kept the new file narrowly scoped to the cliff attack routines rather than leaving the matched code in the broad ft_08A4 translation unit.

Used small static inline helpers for the exact input-bit tests: A/B for cliff attack and LR for the later transition check. This mirrors the assembly's bitmask branches while keeping the condition readable.

Reused existing cliff climb callbacks for cliff attack Anim, Phys, and Coll, matching the assembly wrappers that simply branch through to ftCo_CliffClimb_Anim, ftCo_CliffClimb_Phys, and ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll.
Converted the damage-percent threshold comparison into a clear FtMotionId selection between ftCo_MS_CliffAttackQuick and ftCo_MS_CliffAttackSlow using p_ftCommonData->x488.

Preserved important side effects in the motion setup path: Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftCommon_8007E2F4(fp, 32), setting fp->x221D_b7 and fp->x221D_b5, and calling ftCo_CliffCatch_Phys.
Cleaned up related prototypes so newly matched C code could call ftCo_800DF6F8 and ftCo_800DF72C as bool predicates on ftCo_Fighter* rather than as unknown-parameter stubs.
decomp_lessons: When an action state's callbacks are pure forwarding wrappers, matching them as one-line calls to the sibling state's callbacks can exactly replace assembly wrappers without inventing new behavior.
Input flag tests in Fighter input fields can often be expressed as small inline bool helpers to preserve codegen and make the bitmask meaning searchable.
Motion-state selectors that compare fighter percent against ftCommonData thresholds should be typed with FtMotionId once the target states are known.

Moving matched routines into a named ftCommon state file should be paired with header cleanup: remove stale declarations from aggregate legacy headers and expose the new state through a dedicated header.

For partial decompilation of a larger asm file, add the new C object before the residual asm object in obj_files.mk so the matched symbols are supplied by C while the rest of the asm file remains available.
naming_conventions: New common fighter cliff state files follow the ftCo_CliffAttack.c and ftCo_CliffAttack.h naming pattern under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/.
Action callback names use the state name plus suffixes: ftCo_CliffAttack_Anim, ftCo_CliffAttack_IASA, ftCo_CliffAttack_Phys, and ftCo_CliffAttack_Coll.
Unidentified local helper functions retained address-based names such as ftCo_8009AE14, ftCo_8009AE38, ftCo_8009AEA4, and ftCo_8009AFD4.
The new header guard used the address-style name GALE01_09AE14, consistent with address-anchored guards seen in this codebase slice.
The fighter object typedefs used in this area are ftCo_GObj* for the game object and ftCo_Fighter* for gobj->user_data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The assembly's A/B input mask test on fp->input.x668 was represented as fp->input.x668 & (HSD_PAD_A | HSD_PAD_B).
The assembly's LR input mask test on fp->input.x668 was represented as fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_LR.
The assembly's branch that calls ftCo_8009AEA4 and returns 1 when either the input test or ftCo_800DF6F8 succeeds was expressed as an if condition returning true/false.
The double/float constant sequence used to compare fp->dmg.x1830_percent with p_ftCommonData->x488 was simplified into a C ternary selecting ftCo_MS_CliffAttackQuick versus ftCo_MS_CliffAttackSlow.
The IASA callback matched as an empty function body, corresponding to the assembly's immediate blr.
The sdata2 constants local to the moved routines were removed from the asm file as the C translation supplied the needed constants/codegen.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that name ftCo_8009AE14, ftCo_8009AE38, ftCo_8009AEA4, ftCo_8009AFD4, or ftCo_8009B040 more descriptively.
Search for other cliff state PRs that reuse ftCo_CliffClimb callbacks for attack/wait/catch callback matching.
Check later changes to ft_08A4.h for cleanup of duplicate or stale static declarations around the remaining 09Bxxx routines.
Compare future input predicate matches using fp->input.x668 and HSD_PAD_A/HSD_PAD_B/HSD_PAD_LR for similar inline helper patterns.
